message:
fix(vdbe): apply ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN DEFAULT values for existing rows

When ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN adds a column with a DEFAULT value, existing
rows have fewer columns than the schema expects. The VDBE cursor_column()
was returning NULL for these columns instead of the declared default.

- Add column_defaults_by_root_page and cursor_root_pages to VdbeEngine
- cursor_column() now checks defaults when col_idx >= record width
- OpenRead/OpenWrite populate cursor→root_page mapping
- Connection builds evaluated defaults map from schema and passes to engine
- Un-ignore conformance_032 tests that now pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
